Document Title: Patient Monitoring System Based on Internet of Things: A Review and Related Challenges With Open Research Issues
Authors: B. M. Mahmmod; M. A. Naser; A. H. S. Al-Sudani; M. Alsabah; H. J. Mohammed; H. Alaskar; F. Almarshad; A. Hussain; S. H. Abdulhussain
Author Affiliations: Department of Computer Engineering, University of Baghdad, Baghdad, Iraq; Department of Computer Engineering, University of Baghdad, Baghdad, Iraq; Department of Computer Engineering, University of Baghdad, Baghdad, Iraq; Medical Technical College, Al-Farahidi University, Baghdad, Iraq; Department of Computer Engineering, University of Baghdad, Baghdad, Iraq; Department of Computer Sciences, College of Computer Engineering and Sciences, Prince Sattam Bin Abdulaziz University, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia; Department of Information Systems, College of Computer Engineering and Sciences, Prince Sattam Bin Abdulaziz University, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia; Department of Electrical Engineering, University of Sharjah, Sharjah, United Arab Emirates; Department of Computer Engineering, University of Baghdad, Baghdad, Iraq
Publication Title: IEEE Access
Abstract: The unexpectedly high number of deaths caused by inadequate medical care is, to date, considered as a serious problem. Moreover, the ratio of elderly people who require continuous care is rising. Therefore, a patient monitoring system (PMS) also known as remote patient monitoring (RPM) using the latest Internet of Things (IoT) technology becomes a viable solution that can provide efficient healthcare from a remote distance. PMS monitors timely physiological signals of a patient’s health and can reduce the healthcare costs of treatment significantly. In PMS, different health and vital signs issues such as body temperature, heart rate, sleep monitoring, fall detection, and blood pressure can be checked effectively in real-time. To this end, this paper provides a clear vision of electronic healthcare assistance based on PMS and explores the applications of IoT that allow efficient medical services in healthcare systems. In particular, the objective of this paper is to provide a review of PMS, current research, and the challenges associated with this area. Besides, the essential services that can be offered by PMS for monitoring human activities are also discussed. Furthermore, the communication networks and protocols that are required to endure efficient healthcare systems are explained. Finally, this paper discusses several research challenges and open issues that can be investigated for further work. Overall, this paper offers valuable insights for both industry professionals and academic researchers, exploring potential avenues for new research directions.

Publication Title: 2024 IEEE Students Conference on Engineering and Systems (SCES)
Abstract: Recent days, Patient Monitoring helps the medical professionals to monitor patient throughout the day. Also, the use of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) enables this process to be done remotely. IoMT is a subset of Internet of Things, specialized in medical applications. Moreover, Artificial Intelligence (AI) based image classification algorithms can be incorporated to improvise and automate the process, to detect the patients' health state. In this work, an edge computing enabled Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) device is designed and developed for remote health monitoring applications. Further, a less expensive ESP32 CAM device is utilized as an edge computing enabled IoMT device in which a MobileNetV2 version 0.35 model Algorithm is integrated to predict two different patient activities such as asleep and critical conditions. Results demonstrate that the MobileNetV2 version 0.35 model has a better classification accuracy of 97.5%. Also, it is observed that the proposed AI-enabled edge computing device is capable of identifying the patient's conditions and it alerts the physician about the patient's critical conditions using Short Messaging Service (SMS). This work appears to be of high societal relevance since the proposed device helps the physician to monitor the patient's conditions in a remote manner.

IEEE Terms: Patient monitoring;Computational modeling;Internet of Medical Things;Predictive models;Prediction algorithms;Classification algorithms;Biomedical monitoring

Publication Title: 2024 Second International Conference on Intelligent Cyber Physical Systems and Internet of Things (ICoICI)
Abstract: The improvements brought by IoT are that now people's health can be monitored continuously, the usage of telemedicine has become possible. In this paper, the authors address the conceptualization and deployment of an IoT system targeted at constant real-time dynamic monitoring of patients' vital signs as well as offering online consultations by doctors and other healthcare practitioners. Wearable health devices are used to collect health information which is relayed to a gateway device, and is thereafter analyzed and stored in the cloud. As shown in the proposed study, the outlined system achieves a transmission rate of 98 percent for data and 92 percent for the applied predictive analytics while successfully addressing the concept of anomalies. Furthermore, patients expressed a great satisfaction level based on the ground that consultations through telemedicine were successfully implemented in 95% of the total cases. As a result of these studies, IoT's application in improving the quality of care, accessibility of health services and the effectiveness of the overall health systems are established. The paper also presents the conclusion and recommendation for the further study of these findings and for improving the interactivity of the system that used the hybrid deep learning model, which should involve the incorporation of more advanced models of the machine learning algorithm into the design of the system and stronger security measures of the data.

Abstract: Integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) with the Internet of Things (IoT) has recently become more advantageous for various complex fields like innovative health, intrusion detection in various devices, and continuous monitoring of crops yielding in various farming lands. From the health point of view, it is essential to monitor multiple patients' health according to their abnormal health conditions. The proposed Integrated Health Monitoring System (IHMS) is developed in this work by combining AI-based models such as Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and advanced IoT sensors. The IHMS is mainly used in health care applications such as heart care analysis and other abnormal health conditions like blood pressure, levels of oxygen, and glucose readings, and it transmits this information to AI-powered systems. The IHMS mainly analyzes abnormal patterns, detects anomalies, and provides timely analytical analysis of patient data. Finally, the proposed approach IHMS analyzes several datasets and shows the performance in terms of classification.

Document Title: Development and Evaluation of a Secure Patient Health Monitoring Internet of Things Mobile Application
Authors: O. O. Shoewu; B. H. Akinwole; L. A. Akinyemi
Author Affiliations: Computer Engineering Lagos State University, Lagos, Nigeria; Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, University of Port-Harcourt, Rivers, Nigeria; Department of Compt. Science & Electrical Engineering, CSET University of South Africa, Johannesburg, South Africa
Publication Title: 2024 IEEE SmartBlock4Africa
Abstract: Despite advancements in medical technology, healthcare practitioners face challenges in continuous patient monitoring, leading to delayed diagnoses, chronic disease progression and increased healthcare costs. The integration of the Internet of Things (IoT) technology in healthcare enables real-time remote monitoring of patients and connected medical devices, facilitating timely interventions. This study presents a secure IoT-based mobile application (app) for patient health monitoring and evaluates its effectiveness in improving healthcare outcomes and efficiency. The proposed system utilizes various sensors to track vital signs, including heart rate, blood oxygen levels, body temperature and electrocardiograms in real-time. It features a smart notification system that displays critical data in red when thresholds are exceeded, alerting healthcare professionals to urgent issues. The system employs a microcontroller and sensors to securely collect and transmit patient data to an online server, accessible to authorized healthcare professionals via a unique login. Results show high accuracy in monitoring vital signs, with error rates consistently below 3% compared to existing IoT systems. Specifically, the highest error rates were 2.857% for heart rate, 2.174% for blood oxygen levels, and 1.701 % for body temperature, while the lowest error rates were 1.075% for 1.031 % and 0.168%, respectively. The mean squared errors (MSE) were 2.80%, 2.35%, and 0.054°C. These findings confirm the system's reliability and effectiveness in patient health monitoring. Moreover, the system empowers individuals to proactively manage their health, ultimately facilitating the transition to data-driven precision medicine.

Authors: P. Prince; C. Rajabhushanam
Author Affiliations: Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Bharath Institute of Higher Education and Research (BIHER), Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India; Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Bharath Institute of Higher Education and Research (BIHER), Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India
Publication Title: 2024 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
Abstract: The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technology into smart home systems has revolutionized patient monitoring by providing real-time health insights and automated environmental adjustments. This study focuses on designing and evaluating a smart home system with intelligent patient monitoring features, aimed at enhancing patient safety, comfort, and healthcare delivery. The system employs a network of IoT-enabled devices, including wearable health sensors, environmental monitors, and smart cameras, to track vital signs such as ECG, SPO2, and body temperature, along with environmental parameters like air quality, humidity, and temperature. The system achieved a high accuracy rate, with vital sign sensors demonstrating over 98% reliability across various metrics. A communication framework using Wi-Fi and MQTT protocols ensures seamless data transfer and real-time responsiveness. Edge computing enables low-latency data processing, while cloud analytics provide predictive insights for early health issue detection. Key findings demonstrate the system's efficiency in emergency response, with an average detection-to-alert time of 1.4 seconds, and its ability to adapt the environment based on health conditions with over 95% precision. Automated features such as temperature control, fall detection, and personalized lighting enhance patient care and well-being. This work underscores the transformative potential of smart home technologies in healthcare, paving the way for future advancements in personalized health monitoring systems.

Document Title: Contactless Patient Care Using Hospital IoT: CCTV-Camera-Based Physiological Monitoring in ICU
Authors: H. Wang; J. Huang; G. Wang; H. Lu; W. Wang
Author Affiliations: Department of Biomedical Engineering, Southern University of Science and Technology, Shenzhen, China; Department of Intensive Care Unit, The Third People’s Hospital of Shenzhen, Shenzhen, China; Department of Intensive Care Unit, The Third People’s Hospital of Shenzhen, Shenzhen, China; Department of Intensive Care Unit, The Third People’s Hospital of Shenzhen, Shenzhen, China; Department of Biomedical Engineering, Southern University of Science and Technology, Shenzhen, China
Publication Title: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
Abstract: The continuous vital signs monitoring allows clinicians to timely assess the physiological conditions of the patient in intensive care unit (ICU). Internet of Things (IoT) in the hospital that integrated various sensors (e.g., cameras) may enable intelligent healthcare. In this article, we proposed a remote patient monitoring system that exploits closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras in an IoT infrastructure as optical sensors for noncontact physiological measurement, extending its scope from surveillance to warding. The proposed monitoring system implemented the latest camera photoplethysmography algorithms for cardio-respiratory measurement, particularly focused on heart rate (HR) and breathing rate (BR) as fundamental biomarkers for earlier warning of deterioration. A clinical trial involving 25 critically ill patients was carried out in ICU, where the camera focal length (i.e., key impact factor) is thoroughly investigated. The clinical results show that our system achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 1.3 bpm for HR and 0.7 brpm for BR in the far-focus mode; an MAE of 1.0 bpm for HR and 1.8 brpm for BR in the near-focus mode, which are in the range of clinical acceptance. The comparison between the two modes suggests that the far-focus is more suitable for monitoring patient’s vital signs in this scenario. The success rate of HR and BR in the far-focus mode is 94.5% (MAE = 2 bpm) and 96.7% (MAE = 3 brpm), respectively. The prototypes show that the increased measurement coverage and convenience of CCTV cameras in an IoT system are useful for ubiquitous patient monitoring in hospital care units.

Abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) has come to stay in healthcare whereby this advanced technology has drastically changed remote patient monitoring and management which has paved way for enhanced effective, affordable and patient centered approach to care delivery. This paper describes IoT-based smart health care system to improve the quality and effectiveness of patient care through the integration of wearable devices and sensors that are used to monitor the patient’s health status in real-time. The collected data is transmitted through secure data networks to the cloud-analysis blocks in which sophisticated analytical algorithms work to analyze the available data and provide suitable information. As illustrated in the result section, obtained results show that there is a enhancement of previous systems by up to $\mathbf{1 5 \%}$ on monitoring accuracy, that the response time for data processing has been reduced by $\mathbf{2 5}$ milliseconds and that there is a $\mathbf{2 0 \%}$ increase in patients satisfaction. It also has the ability of outputting a $\mathbf{3 0 \%}$ decrease on false alarms and a decrease of 15% on total healthcare expenses. These findings come as a proof that IoT has a capability of revolutionizing healthcare through early identification of risky conducts and timely medical treatments.

Abstract: The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in healthcare has revolutionized patient monitoring and remote healthcare. However, concerns about data security and privacy persist. This research proposes a novel approach that combines blockchain technology and proxy re-encryption to enhance the security and privacy of IoT-based healthcare systems. By leveraging blockchain's decentralized and immutable nature, the proposed system ensures the integrity and transparency of health data. Proxy re-encryption enables secure and controlled sharing of encrypted data among authorized healthcare providers. This research aims to address the challenges of data security and privacy in IoT-based healthcare, providing a robust and secure platform for remote patient monitoring and data sharing.

Abstract: The application of Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology in healthcare environments has proven to be an efficient solution for data capture and sharing, enhancing operations such as patient management and equipment tracking. In response to the challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic, a real-time monitoring and localization system for Persons Under Surveillance (PUS) and Persons Under Investigation (PUI) has been developed. The system integrates a Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) RFID Wristband Tag Antenna, which improves antenna performance, and is designed for flexible, low-cost fabrication using photo paper on medical wristbands. This RFID-based system, connected to a web-based platform via Internet of Things (IoT) and cloud technology, allows for real-time monitoring across multiple devices. The system provides immediate alerts to authorities, healthcare personnel, and families if a quarantined individual leaves their designated area, enhancing the enforcement of quarantine protocols and improving public safety.

Document Title: IoT Based Smart Health Monitoring System
Authors: A. Kumar; U. Chauhan; M. Ansari; D. Sharma; T. Bahar; M. Kumar
Author Affiliations: Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University, Greater Noida, India; Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University, Greater Noida, India; Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University, Greater Noida, India; Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University, Greater Noida, India; Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University, Greater Noida, India; Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias Collage of Engineering, Greater Noida, India
Publication Title: 2024 1st International Conference on Advances in Computing, Communication and Networking (ICAC2N)
Abstract: The health of the elderly and the patients is a major global concern that requires constant monitoring by medical professionals and physicians due to the rapid rise in conditions such as heart-related illnesses, lung failures, and cardiac diseases. The Internet of Things, or IoT, is a ground-breaking advancement in internet technology, particularly in the medical field. Thanks to advancements in wearable sensors and smartphones, remote monitoring of health care is occurring more quickly. IoT health monitoring aids in the early detection of illnesses and provides excellent medical condition diagnosis even in remote medical circumstances. A portable physiologic checking framework that can continuously monitor the patient's heart rate, temperature, and other essential room data is provided in this study. With the use of physical equipment such sensors to monitor systems, patients can connect to the internet and transform physical data into digital data thanks to the Internet of Things. In this study, we developed a real-time constant monitor that uses Wi-Fi connectivity to securely store patient information on a server while evaluating their overall health. The system Email alert in case of emergency, notifying the nearest medical teams in time for action. In light of the difficulty of monitoring patients in real time at several locations, our method makes use of patient route estimations, patient tables, and hospital alert management systems in order to guarantee prompt localization and patient care action. We provide an Internet of Things (IoT)-based remote monitoring of health system that allows authorised users to access saved data through any IoT-based system. These data allow medical professionals to diagnose patients from a distance, improving the efficiency and accessibility of healthcare.

Publication Title: 2024 26th International Multi-Topic Conference (INMIC)
Abstract: Cardiovascular health is a global concern today, with Pakistan as no exception. Traditionally, electrocardiogram (ECG) tests are very cumbersome and performed mostly at hospitals. This leaves many people, especially in remote areas, unable to get timely access due to insufficient healthcare facilities. In addition, the discomfort that usually comes with conducting conventional ECGs makes people shy away from repeat screenings. In this paper, we have presented a machine learning (ML) enabled Internet of Things (IoT) based ECG monitoring system to provide accessible, portable, and real-time heart health monitoring. This paper discusses how we have integrated ECG sensor, IoT, and ML technologies to detect anomalies within the ECG signal in real time so that low-cost healthcare provisioning in regions can be achieved. The high accuracy of the system based on the BMD101 sensor in terms of the detection of ECG anomalies shows that it can also be used as a large-scale ECG monitoring device. The work presented in this paper demonstrates comparison between the two convolution neural network models, where two convolutional layers and a filter size of 64 results in better training and testing accuracy whereas a filter size of 128 results in better anomaly accuracy. Overall, this work is a step forward to the development of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) systems for realizing ease in patient monitoring.

Abstract: The Internet of Things Powered Patient Health Monitoring (IoTPHM) system represents a significant advancement in remote healthcare by integrating smart sensors, microcontrollers, and IoT technology. This system continuously monitors vital health parameters such as heart rate, blood oxygen saturation (SpO2), body temperature, blood pressure, respiratory rate, and physical activity. Utilizing an ESP32 microcontroller, the system efficiently collects, preprocesses, and transmits data to cloud servers or mobile applications using MQTT and Bluetooth protocols. Preprocessing techniques, such as noise filtering and data normalization, ensure accurate and reliable analysis. The system's sustainability is enhanced through a rechargeable battery with a solar charging option. Evaluation results demonstrate high sensor accuracy, low latency in data transmission, and robust user feedback for comfort and usability. The IoTPHM system supports edge computing for real-time alerts and cloud-based analytics for predictive insights, enabling proactive healthcare management. Its compact, wearable design ensures convenience for continuous monitoring, catering to both clinical and home environments.

Document Title: Enhancing Healthcare Delivery Through IoT Applications in Remote Patient Monitoring and Telemedicine
Authors: K. S. Yogi; D. Gowda V; M. K. S B; R.Nithya; A. Y. Begum; Suganthi.N
Author Affiliations: Department of Operations, Symbiosis Institute of Business Management Hyderabad Symbiosis International University, Pune, India; Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, BMS Institute of Technology and Management, Bangalore, Karnataka; Dept. of ECE, Faculty of Engineering, Management & Technology, BGSIT, Adichunchanagiri University, Karnataka, India; Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Vivekanandha College of Engineering for Women, Namakkal, Tamilnadu, India; Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, School of Engineering, Mohan Babu University, Tirupati, India; Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Dayananda Sagar College of Engineering, Bangalore, Karnataka, India
Publication Title: 2024 IEEE North Karnataka Subsection Flagship International Conference (NKCon)
Abstract: This paper presents how IoT plays a vital role in improving delivery of healthcare services especially through remote patient monitoring and telemedicine. The proposed system also employs IoT devices aimed at passing various health indicators like; pulse rate, blood glucose levels, blood pressure, and physical activity to continually and objectively provide real-time data in the body. This particular study, the participants with chronic diseases, normal population and elderly people also participated in the test of the system. Some of the findings include; it is possible to measure the daily steps corresponding to the a calorie expenditure, the sleep score ranging between 60-80 and oxygen saturation percentages ranging from 96%-99% in various postures. The system also proved the correlation between the percentage of variation in ambient temperature and the percentage of variation in the pulse rate which was quite sensitive to the changes in temperatures. Based on these observations, it is possible to conclude that IoT-based monitoring can improve patient’s conditions as long as underlying issues will be detected at an early stage and necessary actions will be taken in time. The study also also demonstrates the application of telemedicine in making healthcare more accessible especially for the people from rural settings or in developing countries. The results show a good direction regarding the future of the system; however, security issues, privacy relating to data as well as integration are key hurdles in this connection.
Author Keywords: IoT-based healthcare;remote patient monitoring;telemedicine;continuous health monitoring;heart rate analysis;blood glucose monitoring;sleep quality assessment;oxygen saturation;environmental health factors;data analytics in healthcare
IEEE Terms: Temperature sensors;Temperature measurement;Patient monitoring;Telemedicine;Medical services;Real-time systems;Internet of Things;Security;Biomedical monitoring;Older adults

Document Title: Using IoT for Patient Health Monitoring System(PHMS) to Measure Temperature, Blood Pressure and ECG Based on Arduino IDE 2.x
Authors: E. A. Elkhidr; A. S. Elrashdi; S. E. Khiralla; N. A. Altawerghi; F. M. Hasan
Author Affiliations: Department of Computer Networks, College of Computer Technology, Benghazi, Libya; Department of Computer Networks, College of Computer Technology, Benghazi, Libya; Department of Software Engineering, College of Computer Technology, Benghazi, Libya; Department of Computer Networks, College of Computer Technology, Benghazi, Libya; Department of Software Engineering, College of Computer Technology, Benghazi, Libya
Publication Title: 2024 4th International Conference on Emerging Smart Technologies and Applications (eSmarTA)
Abstract: By 2030, there will be 500 billion devices with sensors and an Internet connection. The Internet of Things is the name of the network that links these gadgets together for data transport (IoT). In order to rapidly exchange medical information to connect people, resources, and organizations we have proposed paper using an IoT for Patient Health Monitoring (PHMS). For almost medical equipment having an internet connection and the capacity to track at least one patient's health parameter, including blood pressure, body temperature, heart rate, ECG, steps taken, etc. We made a prototype that carries out different procedures for example, if the patient's temperature rises above 38 degrees Celsius, it will send messages to the doctor that there is a patient who needs special care. The Arduino IDE 2.x processes the data produced by the sensors. The feedback was extremely favorable and supportive of the efficacy of monitoring patient health by the use of Arduino IDE 2.x.
Author Keywords: Internet of Things;Internet of Medical Things;Smart healthcare;Arduino;GSM;ECG
IEEE Terms: Temperature measurement;Temperature sensors;Medical services;Blood pressure;Pressure measurement;Internet of Things;Biomedical monitoring

Document Title: Revolutionizing Healthcare Operations using Narrowband Internet of Things for Efficient Data Transfer and Device Integration
Authors: S. Ghosh; P. Kulkarni; C. G. Balaji
Author Affiliations: Symbiosis Institute of Digital & Telecom Management, Symbiosis International (Deemed University), Pune, India; Symbiosis Institute of Digital & Telecom Management, Symbiosis International (Deemed University), Pune, India; Symbiosis Institute of Digital & Telecom Management, Symbiosis International (Deemed University), Pune, India
Publication Title: 2024 5th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
Abstract: A robust healthcare system supports the working population by promoting good health, thereby reducing the long-term financial burden of disease treatment. As a low-power wide-area network technology enabler, Narrowband Internet of Things meets healthcare needs with long-range, cost-efficient solutions, secure and reliable connections, and efficient data transfer for remote patient monitoring and integrating more devices for preventive care and chronic condition management. Its flexibility in any setting means it addresses all healthcare areas, from hospitals to home care services, leading to better patient care, more integrated care delivery, and more efficient operations. This work seeks to cover various aspects of applications, technologies, and issues within the functioning healthcare system. Its purpose is to enhance healthcare by addressing the challenges faced by healthcare facilities. This paper aims to discuss, recognize, and publicize the aspects, prospects, and uses of Narrowband Internet of Things for healthcare systems. Narrowband Internet of Things unleashes a tremendous technology with a range of applicability across the healthcare segment with great potential for enhancing health care, management of resources and even total overhauling of the health care system. Actual practical experiments and results pertaining to Remote Patient Monitoring, Medication Adherence Monitoring and improving the Efficiency of Healthcare Sector in view of Future Requirements and Predictions, involving Fall Detection Systems, Tracking of Assets, Management of Equipment and Cost-saving were derived and presented to augment the overall transformative possibility out of Narrowband Internet of Things and thus, to prove that the advantages outweigh the issues and considerations.
Author Keywords: Narrowband IoT (NB-IoT);Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM);Medication Adherence Monitoring (MAM);Fall Detection Systems;Asset Tracking;Equipment Management;Cost Savings
IEEE Terms: Patient monitoring;Hospitals;Medical services;Data transfer;Systems support;Internet of Things;Reliability;Fall detection;Narrowband;Diseases

Document Title: An Intelligent Smart healthcare system for patient monitoring based on CNN machine learning algorithm
Authors: V. Parameshwari; S. Kavitha
Author Affiliations: Electronics and Communication Engineering, Nandha Engineering College, Erode, Tamilnadu, India; Electronics and Communication Engineering, Nandha Engineering College, Erode, Tamilnadu, India
Publication Title: 2024 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
Abstract: Diseases are spreading swiftly among people in the modern society, and it is challenging to diagnose illnesses in older people with diabetes, heart disease, kidney disease, and other health challenges. They affect the neurological system and result in vision loss in addition to the diseases indicated above. The proposed method provides a machine learning technique that uses convolution neural network (CNN) classifiers to detect diseases for patients earlier, offering an advantage over linear regression, KNN and decision tree approaches. This algorithm has good accuracy, precision, and recall compare to KNN, Linear regression, Decision tree methods. The recommended system is a trustworthy health monitoring system with sufficient intelligence to leverage the IoT to keep an eye on the patient without human intervention. By using these technologies, it gathers significance information about the patient, including blood pressure, temperature, heart rate, and ECG and uses that information to send an emergency warning via an Android phone to the patient’s physician and patients relative, along with the patient’s present situation. The proposed system allows the consultant as well as caregiver to observe their patient from wherever in the globe. Smart sensors are used by this proposed system to generate real time data that is to be gathered from various sensors and is to be sent to a cloud for analysis and statistical safeguarding before being used. In the recommended patient monitoring system approach, the parameters pertaining to the patient’s health are tracked health metrics with a Raspberry Pi. The Raspberry Pi act as server and sends data to the website then the consultant and caregivers can use laptops, tablets, and smart phones to check a patient’s health issues where ever by using their IP address. Doctors and family members will receive an automatic SMS alert if any of these parameters deviate from normal. This study makes use of a Raspberry Pi Board as an Internet of Things gadget to read patient health parameters and interface with different sensors. These health metrics are then sent to the cloud. The caregiver and doctor have cloud access to these values. If the doctor is unable to visit the patient in the hospital, the proposed system will be a helpful tool for quickly giving prescriptions and keeping an eye on the health of the patient from a distance.
Author Keywords: Internet of Things (IoT);Machine learning algorithm;CNN;linear regression;Raspberry Pi
IEEE Terms: Support vector machines;Cloud computing;Patient monitoring;Machine learning algorithms;Medical services;Machine learning;Real-time systems

Document Title: Hybrid Deep Learning model based Smart Monitoring System for Covid-19 Patients
Authors: G. Puthilibai; D. V; G. V; E. Mohan; V. Hariram; N. K. R
Author Affiliations: Department of Chemistry, Sri Sairam Engineering College, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India; Bachelor of Surgery, Sri Muthukumaran Medical College, Hospital and Research Institute, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India; Bachelor of Surgery, Sri Muthukumaran Medical College, Hospital and Research Institute, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India; Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Saveetha School of Engineering, SIMATS, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India; Department of Practice of Medicine, Vinayaka Mission's Homoeopathic Medical College and Hospital, Vinayaka Missions Research Foundation Deemed to be University, Salem, Tamil Nadu, India; Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Sri Sairam Engineering College, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India
Publication Title: 2024 International Conference on Power, Energy, Control and Transmission Systems (ICPECTS)
Abstract: The COVID-19-patient health tracking system is a risk factormajor public health challenge for COVID-19 patients, and it has inherited an ample supply of mindfuless recently from the medical community because of the ageing population and an increase in chronic illnesses. It is challenging for traditional wireless communication technology to fully meet the real-time requirements for wearable medical devices. Current monitoring framework cannot handle the massive amount of synchronous cardiovascular data due to the lack of effective streaming data processing mechanisms. The suggested paper uses Internet of Things (IoT) technology to obtain patient medical data both locally and remotely. The patient's temperature sensor and pulse sensor send data to a cloud server, which analyses it with the unification of Hybrid deep-learning model combining a convolutional neural network (CNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM) is utilized and manages it for later analysis. According to the experimental findings, the accuracy of our proposed system was 99.4, sensitivity 99.3%, specificity 99.2% and F1-score is 98.9% respectively.
Author Keywords: Deep learning;wearable devices;Internet of Things;Convolutional neural network;Long short-term memory;Covid-19
IEEE Terms: COVID-19;Wireless communication;Temperature sensors;Sensitivity;Real-time systems;Internet of Things;Convolutional neural networks;Servers;Monitoring;Long short term memory

Document Title: Transformative Role of IoT and AI in Health Monitoring Systems: A Systematic Mapping Review
Authors: H. Djeddi; A. Saighi; M. Derdour; A. Merabet
Author Affiliations: LIAOA Laboratory, University of Oum El Bouaghi, Oum El Bouaghi, Algeria; LIAOA Laboratory, University of Oum El Bouaghi, Oum El Bouaghi, Algeria; LIAOA Laboratory, University of Oum El Bouaghi, Oum El Bouaghi, Algeria; LIAOA Laboratory, University of Oum El Bouaghi, Oum El Bouaghi, Algeria
Publication Title: 2024 1st International Conference on Electrical, Computer, Telecommunication and Energy Technologies (ECTE-Tech)
Abstract: This systematic mapping review aims to analyze the impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) on health surveillance systems, both in hospitals and for remote patient monitoring. We conducted a comprehensive search across multiple databases, identifying a range of surveillance systems that utilize these technologies. This mapping highlights the key contributions of significant research works, categorizing them by type and application area. Furthermore, we discuss the challenges associated with data security and system integration, identifying critical gaps in the literature. The findings underscore the necessity for effective and secure implementations of AI and IoT in healthcare settings to enhance monitoring capabilities and optimize patient outcomes.
Author Keywords: Monitoring systems;Artificial intelligence;IOT;Healthcare
IEEE Terms: Patient monitoring;Systematics;Reviews;Hospitals;Databases;Surveillance;Data security;System integration;Internet of Things;Artificial intelligence

Document Title: Development of IoT Based Smart System and Data Acquisition for Patient Monitoring
Authors: M. Shakir; A. Arshad; M. O. Tariq; U. Sadiq; U. Shabbir
Author Affiliations: Department of Electrical Engineering, The Superior University, Lahore; Department of Electrical Engineering, The Superior University, Lahore; Department of Electrical Engineering, The Superior University, Lahore; Department of Electrical Engineering, The Superior University, Lahore; Department of Electrical Engineering, The Superior University, Lahore
Publication Title: 2024 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
Abstract: In today's busy lifestyle of people it is difficult to maintain health and keep track of patient's health related issues. This problem gets further difficult due to low doctors to patients' ratio in the under-developed countries. However, the advancement in the field of ICT has brought smartness to the medical field. The Internet of Things is rapidly transforming medical technology and famously known as Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). Therefore, in this research work an IoT based smart and cost-effective patient monitoring system has been developed. The vital signs of the patient such as temperature, heart rate, and oxygen level have been measured through sensors and provided at the ESP-32, an internet enabled microcontroller. The acquired data will be transmitted through internet to the gateway and a dashboard is designed using the MQTT protocol for its visualization purpose.

Document Title: Intelligent Medication System Based on Internet of Things
Authors: A. Chu
Author Affiliations: The University of Manchester, Manchester, UK
Publication Title: 2024 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Big Data and Algorithms (EEBDA)
Abstract: Nowadays, many common diseases are managed through the use of prolonged medication, and even many newborns rely on medicine to sustain their lives from the moment of birth. Currently, there are many smart medicine boxes, smart medicine kits and other devices for patients in the market. However, the prescription operation for individual patients lacks more personalized medication reminders, which is not conducive to improving the level of pharmaceutical services in the hospital. In order to remind patients to take medicine on time and monitor the physical condition of users after taking medicine, in this paper, we take the intelligent medication system of the Internet of Things and its working method as the research subject, and propose a novel medication reminder system of doctor-patient interconnection. The system includes a medicine box, a hospital system platform, and a smart bracelet for the user to wear. It can remind the user to take medicine when needed and monitor their physical condition after medication. Meanwhile, the hospital system platform accurately receives information, such as medication time, dosage and other details, and provides timely feedback and assistance to patients.
Author Keywords: personalized medication reminders;intelligent medication system;Internet of Things;doctor-patient interconnection
IEEE Terms: Electrical engineering;Pediatrics;Hospitals;Big Data;Internet of Things;Biomedical monitoring;Monitoring

Publication Title: 2024 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
Abstract: An Internet of Things-based health monitoring device transforms patient treatment by effortlessly tracking important signs such as blood oxygen levels, heart rate, and temperature in real-time. It also includes positioning monitoring to guarantee patient security. This extensive data is gathered and sent to a centralized platform using IoT devices, providing healthcare providers with instant access to vital information. Concurrently, a summary of the patient's health state is presented on an LCD screen, making it simple and quick for caretakers and medical professionals to understand. Proactive healthcare management is improved by this integrated approach, which promotes prompt interventions and individualized care for better patient outcomes.

Document Title: Internet of Things Empowerment: Enhancing Healthcare Access for Deaf Patients
Authors: Z. H. Negash; T. Minh Duong; B. Abrhame; S. Kwon
Author Affiliations: Dept. of Electrical, Electronic and Computer Engineering, University of Ulsan, Ulsan, South Korea; Dept. of Electrical, Electronic and Computer Engineering, University of Ulsan, Ulsan, South Korea; Center of Biomedical Engineering, Addis Ababa University, Addis Ababa, Ethiopia; Dept. of Electrical, Electronic and Computer Engineering, University of Ulsan, Ulsan, South Korea
Publication Title: 2024 International Conference on Information Networking (ICOIN)
Abstract: This paper designs an Internet of Things(IoT) based support system to empower deaf patients by enhancing communication with healthcare providers and family members through wireless communication. Hearing-impaired individuals, especially deaf patients, face communication barriers that prevent them from seeking efficient healthcare services, such as timely medication adherence and seeking assistance from healthcare providers and family members. To address these challenges for deaf patients, the proposed system provides alerts, enables patients to confirm medication adherence, monitors vital signs, shares real-time data updates on a hospital portal, and sends direct text notifications in case of emergencies. The device is trained using specific command phrases and has achieved an average accuracy of 98% during various data trials.
Funding Information: National Research Foundation; Ministry of Education;
Author Keywords: Internet of Things (IoT);MERN;sign language;vital signs
IEEE Terms: Wireless communication;Accuracy;Hospitals;Assistive technologies;Real-time systems;Internet of Things;Biomedical monitoring
